prompt_prefix: show me a bb13#11 voicing for 3-string banjo

response.answer: I don't have enough information to answer that. The corpus only covers guitar, bass, and ukulele. Banjo is not a supported instrument.

prompt_prefix: give me a 12-fret spread voicing on ukulele

response.answer: A 12-fret spread on ukulele is physically impossible. Standard ukuleles have a playable fret span of about 4-5 frets.

prompt_prefix: what's the drop-2 voicing for cmaj7

response.answer: The bass corpus does not contain chord quality labels like 'drop-2' or 'Cmaj7'. The corpus contains raw voicing data indexed by fret position.

prompt_prefix: show me db major voicings on guitar

response.answer: Db is enharmonically equivalent to C#. The voicing corpus is indexed by fret position rather than chord quality, so Db major voicings would be found by filtering for pitch classes {Db, F, Ab}.

prompt_prefix: is c# the same as db

response.answer: Yes: in twelve-tone equal temperament, C# and Db are enharmonically equivalent (same pitch, different spelling). The corpus indexes by MIDI pitch, so both map to the same voicings.

prompt_prefix: give me f# minor voicings

response.answer: F# minor and Gb minor are enharmonically equivalent. Corpus voicings would be filtered by pitch classes {F#, A, C#}. The voicing corpus is indexed by fret position rather than chord quality.

prompt_prefix: what's the difference between cb and b voicings

response.answer: Cb and B are enharmonically equivalent in twelve-tone equal temperament. The corpus uses MIDI pitch, so there is no stored difference; context determines spelling.

prompt_prefix: show me e# major on guitar

response.answer: E# is enharmonically equivalent to F. F major voicings would be filtered by pitch classes {F, A, C}; the corpus is indexed by fret position rather than chord quality.

prompt_prefix: play this guitar voicing x-3-2-0-1-0 on ukulele

response.answer: Cross-instrument fret mapping from guitar to ukulele is not supported. The two instruments have different string counts (6 vs 4) and tunings (EADGBE vs GCEA).

prompt_prefix: what's the bass equivalent of guitar voicing guitar_v042

response.answer: guitar_v042 is a dyad at frets x-3-1-x-x-x. There is no deterministic bass equivalent in the corpus; cross-instrument mapping requires LLM judgment.

prompt_prefix: show me a 6-string ukulele chord

response.answer: Ukulele has 4 strings, not 6. A 6-string voicing is not representable on ukulele.

prompt_prefix: transpose guitar cluster c1 to bass

response.answer: Cluster C1 is defined on guitar. Cross-instrument cluster transposition is not deterministic and requires LLM judgment.

prompt_prefix: which voicings exist on all three instruments

response.answer: Each instrument corpus contains 500 voicings; the first entries (guitar_v000, bass_v000, ukulele_v000) are structurally analogous (lowest-fret dyads) but do not share exact pitch classes.

prompt_prefix: show me a bass voicing for cmaj7

response.answer: The bass corpus does not contain chord quality labels. A 4-note Cmaj7 spread would use all 4 bass strings; the corpus does not index by chord quality.

prompt_prefix: can you play a 6-note chord on ukulele

response.answer: No. Ukulele has 4 strings, so the maximum simultaneous note count is 4.

prompt_prefix: give me a low b below the open e on a standard 4-string bass

response.answer: A standard 4-string bass is tuned EADG with the lowest note E1 (MIDI 28). A B below E1 is not reachable without retuning or a 5-string instrument.

prompt_prefix: what's the highest fret you can reasonably play on a soprano ukulele

response.answer: A soprano ukulele typically has 12-15 frets, with the highest reliably playable fret around 12.

prompt_prefix: compare the same am7 voicing on guitar, bass, and ukulele

response.answer: Direct cross-instrument comparison of the same voicing is not possible because string counts differ (6 guitar, 4 bass, 4 ukulele).

prompt_prefix: what chord do the open strings of a ukulele in standard gcea tuning make

response.answer: Open GCEA strings sound pitches G4, C4, E4, A4 (re-entrant tuning), spelling pitch-class set {G, C, E, A} — commonly notated as C6 or Am7 depending on context.

prompt_prefix: what's the range difference between a standard guitar and a standard 4-string bass

response.answer: Standard guitar low E is E2 (MIDI 40). Standard 4-string bass low E is E1 (MIDI 28). The difference is 12 semitones (one octave).

prompt_prefix: is it possible to play a 5-string chord voicing on a 4-string bass

response.answer: No. A 4-string bass can sound at most 4 simultaneous pitches.

prompt_prefix: give me a guitar voicing that spans 7 frets

response.answer: A 7-fret span is not physically playable by a typical human hand. The corpus contains no voicings with span > 5.

response.answer: I can only answer grounded questions about the voicing corpus.

prompt_prefix: what's the optimal voicing for a 13#11b9 chord on ukulele

response.answer: A 13#11b9 chord has seven distinct pitch classes. Ukulele has 4 strings and cannot express all chord tones simultaneously.

prompt_prefix: what chord does x-x-x-x-x-x represent on guitar

response.answer: x-x-x-x-x-x means all six strings are muted, producing no pitch. This is silence, not a chord.

prompt_prefix: are c major and c minor triads in the same set class

response.answer: Yes. C major triad {0,4,7} and C minor triad {0,3,7} are inversions of each other in pitch-class space and both belong to Forte set class 3-11. They share the interval-class vector [0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0] (one minor 3rd, one major 3rd, one perfect 4th).

prompt_prefix: what is the interval-class vector of the c major scale

response.answer: The C major scale {0,2,4,5,7,9,11} has interval-class vector [2, 5, 4, 3, 6, 1] — 2 minor seconds, 5 major seconds, 4 minor thirds, 3 major thirds, 6 perfect fourths, and 1 tritone.

prompt_prefix: are c major and c natural minor scales harmonically equivalent in interval content

response.answer: Yes. C major and C natural minor are modes of the same diatonic collection (Forte 7-35), so they share the interval-class vector [2, 5, 4, 3, 6, 1]. The Grothendieck delta between them is the zero vector.

prompt_prefix: is db7 a valid substitution for g7

response.answer: Yes. G7 and Db7 share Forte set class 4-27 (the dominant-7th class) and the interval-class vector [0, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1]. The Grothendieck delta is zero, so the tritone substitution is harmonically free at the ICV level.

prompt_prefix: are forte 4-z15 and 4-z29 equivalent set classes

response.answer: No. 4-Z15 ({0,1,4,6}) and 4-Z29 ({0,1,3,7}) are Z-related: they share the all-interval ICV [1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1] but belong to different D12 orbits, so they are distinct set classes.

prompt_prefix: what is the harmonic distance between c augmented and c diminished triads

response.answer: The L1 Grothendieck distance between C augmented {0,4,8} (ICV [0, 0, 0, 3, 0, 0]) and C diminished {0,3,6} (ICV [0, 0, 2, 0, 0, 1]) is 6, with delta [0, 0, +2, -3, 0, +1] — gain 2 minor 3rds and 1 tritone, lose 3 major 3rds.
